coverage_notes: Worked entirely from staged data (6 live HTML captures + full component/CSS source). No live MCP call attempted — Shopify/Semrush/Vercel/Sentry connectors were flagged as failing and none were needed: every finding is verifiable from file:line or capture excerpts. Could NOT check: real rendered layout at specific viewport widths (no browser/screenshot tooling — all responsive findings are derived from CSS media-query math and DOM order, which a dev can reproduce in devtools in minutes); live PostHog funnels to quantify the 881-1024px tablet share or PLP CLS impact; the cart PAGE capture (only cart drawer + empty states reviewed from code); checkout (hosted by Shopify, out of scope). Homepage first-viewport hierarchy, PDP buy-box ordering (price -> financing -> variants -> ATC -> trust), blog-archive scannability, footer size (35 links), and cart/wishlist/compare empty states were all reviewed and found genuinely good — no filler findings reported for them. The dead 'popup' newsletter source was given as known context and is not re-reported.

findings.title: Tablet buy-flow dead zone (881-1024px): sticky ATC bar opens an invisible variant sheet and locks page scroll
findings.evidence: app/globals.css:2845 shows `@media (max-width: 1100px) { .pdp-sticky-bar { display: block; } }` but app/globals.css:2891-2893 hides the sheet it opens: `@media (min-width: 881px) { .pdp-variant-scrim, .pdp-variant-sheet { display: none; } }`. The stale comment at globals.css:2848-2851 claims the bar is '<=880px', which is wrong. buy-box.tsx:345 wires the bar CTA to `setSheetOpen(true)` for any multi-option product, and sticky-variant-sheet.tsx:64 runs `useBodyScrollLock(open)` unconditionally. The PDP grid stacks at <=1024px (globals.css:2656), so on 881-1024px viewports (iPad Pro 12.9 portrait = 1024, most Android tablets, half-snapped laptop windows) the bar appears on scroll; tapping 'Add to cart' opens a display:none sheet while the scroll lock and pdp-sheet-open chat-hide (globals.css:604) still fire.
findings.impact: Shoppers on ~881-1024px viewports who scroll a multi-option PDP (every mattress with sizes) and tap the sticky Add-to-cart get a frozen, unscrollable page with no visible dialog, no scrim, and no touch escape (only Esc key closes it). This is a hard add-to-cart blocker on the highest-intent template for the tablet segment.
findings.proposed_fix: Align the two breakpoints: either gate `.pdp-sticky-bar { display:block }` to max-width 880px, or change the sheet/scrim hide to `min-width: 1101px`. One-line CSS change either way; also fix the stale comment at globals.css:2848-2851. Add a defensive guard in BuyBox: don't `setSheetOpen(true)` when `matchMedia('(min-width:881px)')` matches (or make the sheet always renderable at any width the bar can show).

findings.title: `--container-pad` token is used 5x but never defined — padding collapses to 0 on the PLP content block, PLP sibling nav, mobile announcement bar, HTML sitemap, and admin dashboard
findings.evidence: Repo-wide grep finds zero definitions of `--container-pad` (globals.css :root at lines 83-87 defines only --container/--gutter/--top-bar-h/--nav-h). Usages: globals.css:336 `.announcement-bar { padding: 6px var(--container-pad); }` (mobile <=640px), :3851 `.plp-sibling-nav { padding: var(--s-5) var(--container-pad) 0; }`, :3904 `.plp-content { padding: 0 var(--container-pad); }`, :5814 `.html-sitemap`, :5856 `.dashboard`. Per CSS spec, var() referencing an undefined custom property makes the whole declaration invalid-at-computed-value-time -> the padding shorthand resets to its initial value (0 on all sides, including the vertical 6px on the announcement bar). The PLP capture confirms `.plp-content` is a top-level <section>, not nested inside a padded `.container` (plp-latex-mattresses.html: `</section><section class="plp-content" aria-labelledby="plp-content-h-latex-mattresses">`).
findings.impact: On every viewport under 1200px, the SEO/content block that renders on all 77 collection pages ('About latex mattresses...', FAQ, buying-guide links) and the cross-cut sibling nav run flush against the screen edge — edge-to-edge text on the primary browse template for the majority-mobile audience. The sale announcement bar on phones loses all padding, so '4th of July Mattress Sale...' touches the viewport edges. Reads as broken/unfinished on the two surfaces most shoppers see first.
findings.proposed_fix: Define the token once in :root, e.g. `--container-pad: var(--gutter);` (32px desktop, 20px <=768px follows automatically since --gutter is already re-declared there), or replace the 5 usages with var(--gutter). Verify .plp-content/.plp-sibling-nav/.html-sitemap then match the .container gutter rhythm.

findings.title: Financing math contradicts itself between PLP card and PDP: same mattress shows 'From $96/mo' on the card and 'From $191.58/mo with Affirm' on the product page
findings.evidence: PLP card uses Synchrony 24-month math: lib/financing-calc.ts:16 `FINANCING_DEFAULT_MONTHS = 24`, rendered in plp-card.tsx:102-106 as 'From $X/mo · 0% APR' (no provider named). Live capture plp-latex-mattresses.html shows the $2,299-$4,399 Diamond Lucille card with 'From $96/mo · 0% APR' ($2299/24). The PDP line directly under the price divides by 12 instead: pdp-financing-line.tsx:42 `const monthly = amount / 12;` rendered as 'From $X/mo with Affirm' — $2,299/12 = $191.58/mo, double the card's figure. plp-card.tsx:41-44 comment even claims the card 'Mirrors the PDP/cart financing callout so the affordance reads consistently across browse -> product -> cart' — the opposite is true. PDP capture confirms the /12 math: '$259 ... From $21.58 /mo with Affirm' (pdp-standard-foundation-box-spring.html).
findings.impact: The monthly price doubles at the exact click where the shopper moves from browse to decision, on $1,500+ mattresses where financing is the stated ATC-drop remedy (pdp-financing-line.tsx:16-18 cites a 94% PDP->ATC drop). Feels like bait-and-switch and invites distrust of every other price claim on the site.
findings.proposed_fix: Pick one canonical monthly framing per price band and use it on both templates: either quote Synchrony 24-mo 0% APR on both (add the same 'with Affirm'/'0% APR' provider label), or pass months/provider from a single shared helper (financing-calc.ts) into both PlpCard and PdpFinancingLine so the numbers can never diverge.

findings.title: Compare tray floats on top of the mobile sticky Add-to-cart bar on PDPs (z-80 over z-60, both bottom-anchored) — chat bubble was lifted for this exact case, the tray wasn't
findings.evidence: globals.css:432-437 `.compare-tray { position: fixed; bottom: 16px; z-index: 80; }` vs globals.css:2836 `.pdp-sticky-bar { position: fixed; inset: auto 0 0 0; z-index: 60; }`. The tray renders on PDPs: compare-store.ts:60 `if (pathname.startsWith('/products/')) return true;` in isShoppingRoute, used by compare-tray.tsx:56. The chat bubble got a dedicated lift for this collision (globals.css:599-601 `body.pdp-atc-visible .chat-bubble { bottom: calc(85px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom)); }`) but no equivalent rule exists for `.compare-tray` (no `pdp-atc-visible .compare-tray` selector anywhere in globals.css).
findings.impact: A shopper who compared 1-4 mattresses on the PLP and taps into a PDP gets the compare pill (up to ~100vw-32px wide, centered) rendered directly over the sticky bar's product title/price and, on narrow phones, over part of the Add-to-cart button — during the exact scroll state the sticky bar exists to serve. Compare users are the highest-intent segment.
findings.proposed_fix: Mirror the chat fix: add `@media (max-width:1100px) { body.pdp-atc-visible .compare-tray { bottom: calc(85px + env(safe-area-inset-bottom)); } }` (the body class is already maintained by buy-box.tsx:147-150), or hide the tray on PDPs while the sticky bar is on.

findings.title: Chat FAB (z-90) floats over the PLP filter bottom-sheet (z-60), covering the right end of the full-width 'Show results' button
findings.evidence: globals.css:570-574 `.chat-bubble { position: fixed; right: 16px; bottom: 16px; z-index: 90; }` vs globals.css:4302-4311 `.plp-filters { position: fixed; inset: auto 0 0 0; ... z-index: 60; }` (mobile <=880px) and :4357-4365 `.plp-filters-mobile-foot .btn { width: 100%; }` — the sheet's sticky apply footer. grep for any chat-hide/body-class in app/_components/plp-filters/ returns no matches; chat-widget.tsx:26 only hides on /admin and /account. The team already recognized this bug class on the PDP variant sheet — globals.css:602-604 `body.pdp-sheet-open .chat-bubble { opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; }` — and the cart drawer avoids it by z-200 (globals.css:3352). The PLP filter sheet got neither treatment.
findings.impact: On phones, opening Filters on any collection page leaves the 56px chat bubble sitting on top of the sheet's footer, overlapping the right side of 'Show results'. Mis-taps open the chat mid-filtering; the primary apply action is partially obstructed on the template where filtering IS the shopping activity.
findings.proposed_fix: Toggle a body class from the filter sheet open state (same pattern as buy-box.tsx:151-154) and add `body.plp-filters-open .chat-bubble { opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; }`, or raise `.plp-filters`/scrim above z-90 like the cart drawer.

findings.title: Compare toggle pops in after hydration on every PLP card — 24-card grid reflows ~40px per card under the shopper's thumb
findings.evidence: Live capture plp-latex-mattresses.html contains zero occurrences of `compare-toggle` (grep count 0 across the whole document) while rendering 24 `.pcard.plp-card` articles — because compare-toggle.tsx:54 `if (!hydrated) return null;`. The button participates in normal flow at the card bottom: globals.css:406-413 `.compare-toggle { margin-top: 6px; padding: 8px 0; min-height: 32px; }` (~38-44px of height added per card post-hydration). PlpCard renders it as the last flow child of the article (plp-card.tsx:118). The wishlist heart avoids this only because it is absolutely positioned (globals.css:2013-2014); the compare toggle is not.
findings.impact: Every PLP/search visitor gets a full-grid layout shift when JS hydrates (rows push down ~40px), contributing to the PLP LCP/CLS tails already noted in code comments (plp-card.tsx:73-75 cites PostHog p95 4-6s LCP), and causing mis-taps on slow connections where hydration lands while the user is already scrolling/tapping cards. Compare — a differentiating feature — is also invisible to any shopper before hydration completes.
findings.proposed_fix: Render the toggle server-side in its unselected default state (selection is only readable client-side, but the unselected button markup is static) and reconcile selected state after hydration; or reserve the space with a fixed-height placeholder row in the card so hydration never changes card height.

findings.title: Live 404 serves a blank client-rendered shell — the well-designed branded 404 never arrives as server HTML
findings.evidence:
Capture 404-page.html: the entire served body is `
` under ``, with no stylesheet link in ; the full 404 UI (logo header, 'We couldn't find that page', nf-grid CTA tiles, phone link) exists only inside the RSC flight payload in
findings.impact: Anyone hitting a dead URL (2,029 legacy redirects exist, so stragglers are common) sees a pure white page for the duration of JS download+execution — on mid-tier mobile easily 2-5s — and users with JS blocked/failed see a permanently blank page. First impression from old bookmarks/emails/links is 'the site is down', not the recovery UI the team built.
findings.proposed_fix: Diagnose why the not-found boundary CSR-bails instead of SSR-ing (id=__next_error__ + empty body indicates the 404 is served from the client-side error shell — commonly caused by a dynamic API/searchParams access or missing prerendered /_not-found in the build). Confirm `next build` emits a prerendered /_not-found route and that nothing in the root layout chain forces dynamic rendering for it; the goal is the not-found.tsx markup and CSS link present in the initial HTML response.

findings.title: Breakpoint sprawl (20 distinct media widths, 760px vs 768px near-duplicates) with a documented override regression and a missing design-system doc
findings.evidence: globals.css media queries use ~20 distinct max-width values: 380, 460, 480, 520, 560, 580, 600, 640, 720, 760, 768, 860, 880, 900, 920, 980, 1024, 1100 (grep '@media (max-width'); near-duplicate pairs are both heavily used — 760px in 25 rules vs 768px in 6, and an 860/880/900/920 cluster. The cost is already documented in-file: globals.css:4281-4284 admits an 'earlier 768px-scoped fix existed (line ~1844) but got overridden by this less-specific 880px rule in source order' (a shipped tap-target regression). The tokens header at globals.css:4 points to '/design-handoff/02-design-system.md' which does not exist in the repo (design-handoff/ dir missing), so there is no written breakpoint contract. Concrete user-visible seams: (a) nav collapses to hamburger touch UI at <=1024px (globals.css:2557-2559) but the WCAG 44px tap-target upgrade for icon buttons only applies <=768px (globals.css:2584-2591) — tablets at 769-1024px get touch navigation with the 40px targets the team's own QA note says are 'too small for thumbs'; (b) PLP flips to the mobile filter bottom-sheet at <=880px (globals.css:4278) while the nav/grid flip at 1024px — at 881-1024px shoppers get hamburger nav (mobile mode) plus desktop sidebar filters (desktop mode) on the same screen; (c) the P1 sticky-bar 1100px vs variant-sheet 880px mismatch (ux-pdp-01) is the same failure mode.
findings.impact: Every new component picks its own width and the seams land on real devices (the entire 769-1024px tablet band gets mixed mobile/desktop UI and substandard tap targets). Regressions of this class have already shipped once per the in-file comment, and review can't catch them because there is no canonical breakpoint set to diff against.
findings.proposed_fix: Define 4-5 canonical breakpoints as documented constants (e.g. 480/768/1024/1200, matching where the nav and PDP grid already flip), migrate the 760->768 and 860/880/900/920->1024 (or 880) clusters template by template, and restore/write the referenced design-system doc with a rule that new media queries must use the canonical set. Fold the tap-target rule into the same breakpoint as the hamburger flip (<=1024px) as the first migration.
